_metadata.description: Self-documenting movie and TV show data store with embedded PowerShell mutation instructions.

instructions.context: When the user provides this JSON file and asks to add or modify records, parse the active log. Do not rewrite the file. Instead, generate the exact PowerShell command string required to execute the change locally based on the syntax defined below. Note: The provided JSON may be a minimized clipboard export (omitting fields like Genre, Runtime, or Actors to save tokens). You can still generate mutation commands for all available fields. IMPORTANT: Every entry in this log is a movie the user has already watched. The Status field makes this explicit — 'watched_no_date' means the user watched it before this log was started and the exact date is unknown, NOT that it is unwatched or on a wish list. Only 'want_to_watch' entries are unseen.
instructions.field_notes: Status is one of: 'watched' (seen, WatchDate contains the date(s)), 'watched_no_date' (seen before this log was started — treat as watched, exact date unknown), 'want_to_watch' (not yet seen). WatchDate is an array of YYYY-MM-DD strings (one per viewing), supporting re-watches; empty [] when Status is watched_no_date or want_to_watch. PriorWatch is a boolean: false = genuine first-ever viewing, true = the user had seen this movie before (pre-system or prior watch not logged here), null/absent = unknown. Runtime is zero-padded HH:MM:SS. ReleaseDate is YYYY-MM-DD.

syntax.add_entry: Add-MovieEntry -Title 'Movie Title' -Status 'watched|watched_no_date|want_to_watch' -Rating 5 -WatchDate 'YYYY-MM-DD','YYYY-MM-DD' -PriorWatch $false -ReleaseDate 'YYYY-MM-DD' -Runtime 'HH:MM:SS' -Genre 'Genre1, Genre2' -Director 'Director Name' -Studio 'Studio Name' -Actors 'Actor 1, Actor 2' -Notes 'Optional notes'
syntax.update_property: Update-MovieEntry -Title 'Movie Title' -Property 'WatchDate' -Value 'YYYY-MM-DD'  (for WatchDate this APPENDS the date to the existing list; an empty value clears it; for PriorWatch use $true or $false)
syntax.mark_watched: Mark-MovieWatched -Title 'Movie Title' [-WatchDate 'YYYY-MM-DD'] [-PriorWatch $true|$false]  (atomically sets Status to watched and appends the date; defaults to today if WatchDate is omitted; PriorWatch inferred from prior status when possible)
syntax.audit_entry: Get-MovieEntry -SearchTerm 'Movie Title' [-Detailed] [-Column Title,WatchDate,Status,Rating] [-StatusFilter watched|watched_no_date|want_to_watch] [-Watchlist] [-MinRating N] [-SortBy Title|Year|Rating|WatchDate] [-Descending]
